evidence.text: global exposure and diverse clientele pro cognizant operates in over 40 countries providing consultants with opportunities to work on projects with a global reach this can lead to a diverse and enriching professional experience critical view the complexity of coordinating across multiple time zones and managing cultural differences can be stressful and time-consuming career growth and learning opportunities pro the...
good hr reputation training learning opportunities
progressive leadership and supportive management
variety of roles and learning opportunities to pursue your chosen career path
- fortune 500 company - opportunity for advancement - unlimited pto

extensive global travel opportunities allowing for cultural immersion and diverse client exposure strong collaborative team environment across geographies with dedicated peers who are invested in delivering high-quality work comprehensive benefits package including healthcare paid time off and support for personal development
good health benefits good health benefits good health benefits

great benefits cognizant offers comprehensive benefits including medical dental and vision coverage learning opportunities employees have access to continued education and training which helps in skill development
benefits are reasonably good with health insurance and 401k match
good pay good learning espp 401k insurance

global exposure diverse projects cognizant is a large multinational company with clients across various industries finance healthcare retail etc giving developers opportunities to work on diverse projects and cutting-edge technologies job stability career growth being a well-established it services company cognizant provides decent job security and structured career progression with regular promotions and skill deve...

working at cognizant offers several benefits the company provides global opportunities allowing employees to work on international projects and collaborate with diverse teams there are ample chances for career growth through various training programs helping employees advance their skills and progress in their careers cognizant also offers competitive salaries performance bonuses and comprehensive benefits like heal...

cognizant's many projects provide stability and flexibility in a multitude of categories of business you are likely to find a project that peeks your interest if you are interested great 401k contribution paid time off and work life balance if working remotely
flexible schedule work life balance
work life balance flexibility wfh

i was hired during covid and left on the end of 2022 so not sure if these still apply to this company - i was hired 100 remote - i got a decent productivity bonus and salary increase - i got great colleagues there is quality people here that know their stuff technically so you will get to learn from them - the better consulting company after jumping from tcs - capgemini - cognizant - they also had a great project st...

this was probably the best work environment i ve been apart of the management the team the clinics i worked for all amazing no complaints pay is good benefits are good and the inclusive environment made it easy to work with everyone
great work environment with supportive staff

evidence.location: United States Coast Guard - Air Station Sacramento, United States
evidence.text: its really good and friendly environment
cts offers training opportunities and the ability to work remote
stable job and good benefits
mostly stable job if released can get some projects
job security choice of projects and locations
relocation flexible remote work job security
work culture is good job security is good
pay is not great lots of layoffs
- internal office politics can be seen - when in bench time its upto you to get a project no one rescues you - hikes are very minimum
low pay very dependent on client work conditions

low pay verrrry underpaid basically no upper management no hr contact fear of being laid off if you speak up about low pay had pay raises pushed months back with no warning or reasoning very low mobility or ways to move up
no cons i m not sure about
everything beginning from hr to management

this practice creates a toxic work environment where capable employees are overlooked for promotions and opportunities in favor of less qualified individuals with personal connections it's disheartening to see talent and hard work go unrecognized due to these unprofessional practices additionally the lack of transparency and fairness in decision-making processes further demoralizes employees the company does not see...
questionable leadership at vp and up levels competitive internal politics lack of organization and leadership ownership very little training in consulting practices
lots of junior engineers every assignment had me carrying 1-3 junior engineers very eager to learn but no where near where they needed to be when they started most of them absorbed quickly
leadership changes in 2019 were not helpful recent changes seem to be working for them

- top performers are given more work while slackers barely get any but everyone is paid the same - layoffs take place pretty much annually - every one seems to be burnt out all the time - managers have zero work life balance - leadership is manipulative - hr is a mess that never responds to email - pay raises avg 1 a year regardless of inflation or cost of living increases - leadership prefers to issue updates and c...

project-based pay isn't negotiable not a lot of growth cognizant handles business process outsourcing for other technology companies what i didn't understand at the time of being hired is even though i was a cognizant employee my employment was at the whim of the client we were providing services to because everything from the employment terms hourly rates benefits etc were defined in the contract there's not a lot...
pay is not the best and it's hard to gain raises and bonuses even with receipts for working above and beyond
time off and benefits not so good
the salary is not competitive

- toxic fear-based management constant threats to skill up or else - hiring bias is real u s workers are deprioritized for visa hires - zero career growth promotions are about politics not talent - it policies actively prevent productivity you can t install basic tools - if you quit you become a ghost my manager didn t even tell my team i left - endless pointless argumentation instead of solving problems expect endl...
salary is not that competitive career growth

- too many corrupt people employed least where i was located including hr internal investigations even past ceo s - projects are subject to end immediately without notice this includes the very largest projects in the world regardless of what you are told - pay is horrible for expected work demands - outside of your project hr internal investigations team there is no one to reach out to - potential harassment from v...

no respect for employee contributions abysmal salary increases cognizant generated billions in revenue but it wasnt as many billions as they should have so company wide salary increases were 1 for 2024 constant threat of layoffs and secrecy regarding these they do not want to risk losing worker enthusiasm before they have extracted every drop of productivity from you you are just a number and the rules do not apply...
terrible management racism and discrimination rampant low pay
not great pay masters not good work life balance peer pressure not conducive senior management

management has no interest in helping you further your career blocks you from making internal moves that aren't beneficial to your current practice mba hires beware - hr will not salary match when the new cohorts are paid more than you

highly unprofessional and lack humanity never supported me in terms of immigration due to which i had run out of my student visa and had to go back to my home country they support people who they are on good terms with in india typical desi company attitude

the company underpays and then fails to provide annual increases that keep up with inflation so you will just get poorer and poorer with every passing year last year if you were a top performer you got a 1 5 increase as an example this year if you were a manager you got no increase the indian culture is very much do as i say with a heavy presence in india you are working odd hours to talk to your indian teammates wh...
there is no reason to pursue any opportunities with this company in the us
